summary:
Section 228A of the Indian Penal Code protects individuals from revealing private details related to a crime such as rape by ensuring that medical professionals do not disclose this information without consent. This law aims at preserving the dignity and safety of rape survivors while investigating these serious offenses. \boxed{A}

The summary captures both core aspects—protecting patient/rape survivor’s confidentiality—and aligns with the specific requirement regarding which part of the code addresses punishment for breaching this secrecy. It also stays within two concise statements focusing solely on pertinent elements needed per instructions provided earlier.
